The derive_from/cluster handling: p.registers() flattens registers; clusters of repeated registers may need reg_iter() — if the parser version exposes a different iterator, use it (the existing crates/svd-ingestor/src/lib.rs shows the working pattern — read it for reference).

docs.title: GDMA Peripheral-Coupled Mode Implementation Plan (Slice 3A)
docs.text:
For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (- [ ]) syntax for tracking.

Goal: ESP32-S3 GDMA moves real bytes between memory descriptors and the UART/SPI/I2S peripherals (peripheral-coupled mode), so standard ESP-IDF DMA drivers run instead of receiving phantom EOFs.

Architecture: Add the missing PERI_SEL register pair per channel; in tick_with_bus(), coupled channels pump bytes between descriptor chains and the target peripheral THROUGH THE BUS (UART via its real MMIO FIFO; SPI via its DMA-enabled transaction path; I2S via a new sample-stream surface). Unmodeled peripheral ids keep today's auto-complete fallback (compatibility). EOF/interrupt semantics per direction follow the existing M2M wiring (sources 66–75).

Tech Stack: Rust (labwired-core), register-level integration tests (no cross-toolchain dependency in the gate; e2e firmware optional follow-up).

Spec: parent labwired repo docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-11-hw-substrate-sota-design.md, Slice 3 ("GDMA peripheral-coupled mode"). LCD coupling explicitly deferred.

CRITICAL — workspace: ALL work in the core worktree /home/andrii/projects/labwired/.worktrees/core-slice3-gdma (branch feat/gdma-peripheral-coupling, tracks core origin/main). Another agent works in /home/andrii/projects/labwired and /home/andrii/projects/labwired/core — NEVER touch those. Commits: no Claude/AI/assistant references, no Co-Authored-By.

Ground-truth anchors (read before each task): crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/gdma.rs (958 LoC: M2M walk at 339–397, auto-complete fallback at 294–326, irq emission 431–450, doc-comment contract 79–86), uart.rs (FIFO at offset 0x00, STATUS counts at 0x1C), gpspi.rs (W0..W15 + SPI_CMD.USR immediate-complete + storage-only DMA_CONF 0x30), i2s.rs (control surface only; RXEOF_NUM 0x64), and the M2M tests (gdma.rs 500–959) as the test-style template.

docs.title: Task 0: PERI_SEL ground truth (research gate, no production code)
docs.text:
Files: - Create: docs/esp32s3_gdma_peri_sel.md (short note)

GDMA on real silicon binds channels to peripherals via GDMA_IN_PERI_SEL_CHn_REG / GDMA_OUT_PERI_SEL_CHn_REG. Before implementing, pin down: (a) the register OFFSETS within the per-channel block, and (b) the PERI_IN_SEL/PERI_OUT_SEL value encodings (which integer selects SPI2, SPI3, UHCI0/UART-DMA, I2S0, I2S1, LCD_CAM, AES, SHA, ADC, RMT).

  • [ ] Step 1: Search the repo for existing evidence: grep -rn "PERI_SEL\|peri_sel\|peri_in_sel" crates/ docs/ scripts/ — ROM-thunk or hw-oracle captures may already reference them. Also check any vendored ESP-IDF headers (grep -rln "GDMA_IN_PERI_SEL" --include=*.h . and grep -rn "SOC_GDMA" crates/).
  • [ ] Step 2: If absent in-repo, derive from authoritative public sources (ESP32-S3 TRM chapter "GDMA Controller", or esp-idf soc/esp32s3/include/soc/gdma_struct.h + gdma_channel.h via WebFetch of the espressif GitHub raw files). Record in docs/esp32s3_gdma_peri_sel.md: offsets relative to the channel block (expected: IN_PERI_SEL at 0x40, OUT_PERI_SEL at 0xA0 within the 0xC0 stride — VERIFY, do not trust this parenthetical), the value→peripheral table, and the citation (URL + struct names). Wrong offsets here poison every later task — this is the one task where slowing down pays.
  • [ ] Step 3: Cross-check against the existing register map constants in gdma.rs lines 99–115 (no collisions with already-modeled offsets). Commit: docs(core): ESP32-S3 GDMA PERI_SEL register/value ground truth

docs.title: Task 1: PERI_SEL registers + coupled-mode routing skeleton
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/gdma.rs

TDD. Add IN_PERI_SEL/OUT_PERI_SEL registers (offsets from Task 0) as read/write storage on each direction's state; define

/// Peripheral targets GDMA can couple to. Values per Task 0 ground truth.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
enum DmaPeripheral {
    Spi2, Spi3, Uhci0, I2s0, I2s1, LcdCam, Aes, Sha, Adc, Rmt, // adjust to the verified table
    Unknown(u32),
}
impl DmaPeripheral { fn from_sel(v: u32) -> Self { /* per table */ } }

Routing rule in the link-start paths (replacing the blanket auto-complete at lines 294–326): when MEM_TRANS_EN is clear, look up DmaPeripheral::from_sel(peri_sel): - Coupled set (Uhci0, Spi2, Spi3, I2s0, I2s1): mark the direction pending_coupled (new state) — byte movement happens in tick_with_bus (Tasks 2–4). Do NOT latch EOF at start. - Fallback set (everything else, incl. Unknown): keep today's immediate auto-complete byte-for-byte (the doc comment at 79–86 must be updated to describe the new split). This preserves AES/SHA/ADC-using firmware behavior exactly.

Tests (extend the existing test module style): PERI_SEL read/write round-trip per channel/direction; coupled-set start does NOT latch EOF; fallback-set start still auto-completes (pin existing behavior with an explicit test so the compatibility promise is enforced); M2M tests untouched and green.

Run: cargo test -p labwired-core gdma → green; cargo clippy -p labwired-core -- -D warnings; cargo fmt --all. Commit: feat(core): GDMA PERI_SEL registers + coupled/fallback routing split

docs.title: Task 2: UART (UHCI0) coupling — TX and RX byte movement
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/gdma.rs - Modify (only if needed): crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/uart.rs

On real silicon UART-DMA flows through UHCI0 bridging GDMA↔UART FIFOs. We model the data path, not UHCI framing: a coupled OUT (TX) channel walks its descriptor chain (reuse walk_out_chain) and pushes each byte to the UART by bus.write_u8(UART_FIFO_ADDR, byte) — the UART's real MMIO FIFO write path, so serial output, interrupts, and STATUS counts behave identically to CPU writes. A coupled IN (RX) channel reads STATUS.RXFIFO_CNT via the bus, pops available bytes via bus.read_u8(UART_FIFO_ADDR), and writes them into the IN descriptor chain (reuse walk_in_chain mechanics, but incremental: partial fills must NOT latch IN_SUC_EOF until the descriptor chain is satisfied or the UART idles — model: EOF when the current descriptor fills OR rx fifo empties after at least one byte with IN_DONE per completed descriptor; verify the exact EOF policy against how ESP-IDF's uart driver consumes it — uart_read_bytes waits on IN_SUC_EOF; document the chosen policy in the code comment).

Which UART instance: derive the UART base address from the UHCI0 binding (UHCI0 bridges to UART0 by default; if the repo's uart.rs models multiple instances, couple to UART0's base and document). Respect throughput realism minimally: move at most N bytes per tick (pick the value other peripherals use for batched ticking; a const COUPLED_BYTES_PER_TICK keeps it tunable).

Tests (register-level, in gdma.rs test module or a sibling integration test — follow where the M2M tests live): - TX: program a descriptor with "HELLO", set OUT_PERI_SEL=UHCI0, start OUT link → after ticks, the UART's tx output (however uart.rs exposes transmitted bytes — read its tests for the accessor) contains "HELLO"; OUT_EOF latched only after the last byte entered the FIFO. - RX: preload the UART rx fifo (uart.rs test helper or MMIO loopback — read how uart tests inject rx), program IN descriptor, start IN link → memory contains the bytes; IN_SUC_EOF + irq source 66+n raised; partial-fill does not EOF prematurely. - Both with the interrupt enable mask set, assert explicit_irqs carries the right source ids.

Run gates as Task 1. Commit: feat(core): GDMA↔UART coupled transfers via MMIO FIFO path

docs.title: Task 3: GP-SPI (SPI2/SPI3) coupling
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/gpspi.rs - Modify: crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/gdma.rs

Today gpspi.rs auto-completes a transaction on SPI_CMD.USR using the W0..W15 buffer; DMA_CONF is storage-only. ESP-IDF's SPI master driver in DMA mode: configures GDMA channel (PERI_SEL=SPI2/3), sets DMA_CONF enables, fills descriptors, kicks USR. Model: when USR is kicked with DMA tx/rx enabled in DMA_CONF (read the real bit names from the register doc in gpspi.rs or ESP-IDF spi_struct.h — record them), the SPI defers completion until GDMA supplies/consumes the data: GDMA's coupled OUT chain provides MOSI bytes (replacing W-buffer reads), coupled IN chain receives MISO bytes (the model's existing MISO fill policy: attached device response if a device is attached — gpspi has an attach() path used by the e-paper work — else 0xFF). On completion: SPI latches TRANS_DONE; GDMA latches OUT_EOF/IN_SUC_EOF.

Coupling mechanism: GDMA and SPI are separate peripherals on the bus; the byte handoff cannot go through MMIO (W buffer is the non-DMA path). Implement the smallest cross-peripheral contract that fits the existing architecture — read how the bus owner composes peripherals (crates/core/src/bus/mod.rs) and choose: (a) a shared DmaEndpoint trait object registered by base-address/peri-id that GDMA can resolve during tick_with_bus (mirrors how external device attach() works), or (b) a mailbox in the bus that SPI posts "transaction pending, need N bytes" requests to and GDMA services (mirrors the STM32 DmaRequest pattern at dma.rs:127–246). Prefer whichever pattern the codebase already uses for cross-peripheral signaling — investigate dma_requests in PeripheralTickResult first; if that mechanism can carry this, use it and do NOT invent a new trait. Document the decision in the module doc.

Tests: DMA-mode SPI3 transaction with an attached test device (reuse the e-paper test device pattern from the 93f6f9a8 work or gpspi tests): descriptor-fed MOSI bytes reach the device byte-for-byte; device-fed MISO bytes land in the IN descriptor buffer; TRANS_DONE + both EOFs ordered correctly; non-DMA (CPU W-buffer) transactions unchanged (regression-pin with existing tests).

Run gates. Commit: feat(core): GDMA-coupled SPI2/3 transactions (DMA-mode master path)

docs.title: Task 4: I2S coupling — sample streaming
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/i2s.rs - Modify: crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/gdma.rs

I2S on S3 is DMA-only (no CPU FIFO) — this task makes I2S actually move data for the first time. Scope (minimal faithful): TX path: coupled OUT chain bytes stream into a new I2S sample sink — an internal bounded buffer drained at the configured sample rate is over-modeling; instead expose transmitted samples to tests/observability the same way UART exposes tx bytes (read how uart.rs surfaces output and mirror it; if there's a generic observable mechanism use it). RX path: a test-injectable sample source feeds the IN chain; RXEOF_NUM (i2s.rs 0x64) governs when IN_SUC_EOF latches (that's its documented purpose — wire it for real). TX EOF: when the descriptor chain drains. Start/stop respects the I2S TX/RX start bits already modeled in i2s.rs.

Tests: TX stream of a known pattern lands in the sample sink in order with OUT_EOF after chain completion; RX with RXEOF_NUM = N latches IN_SUC_EOF after exactly N bytes; I2S start-bit gating (no movement while stopped).

Run gates. Commit: feat(core): GDMA-coupled I2S sample streaming (RXEOF_NUM honored)

docs.title: Task 5: Contract docs, compatibility matrix, full verification
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/gdma.rs (module doc 79–86 rewrite) - Modify: the compatibility/支持 matrix doc (docs/specs/compatibility_matrix.md in the parent repo is OUT of this worktree — instead update core's own doc: grep docs/ for the S3/GDMA support statements and update those in THIS repo) - Modify: CHANGELOG.md (core repo conventions — read recent entries)

  1. Rewrite the gdma.rs "What remains unimplemented" section: coupled = UART(UHCI0)/SPI2/SPI3/I2S0/I2S1 with real byte movement; fallback auto-complete = AES/SHA/ADC/RMT/LCD_CAM + unknown (explicit list); LCD_CAM deferred-by-design note.
  2. Sweep for stale claims: grep -rn "auto-complete\|peripheral-coupled\|non-m2m" crates/core/src/peripherals/esp32s3/ docs/ and update every statement that now lies.
  3. Full verification (paste summaries): cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy -p labwired-core -- -D warnings && cargo test -p labwired-core (scoped package — workspace-wide may need cross-compiled fixtures: prebuild cargo build -p firmware-hil-showcase --release --target thumbv7m-none-eabi if running broader suites, per the known strict_onboarding requirement).
  4. Confirm no regression in the existing S3 e2e tests (cargo test -p labwired-core --test e2e_labwired_ereader and any other S3 e2e — the e-paper CPU-driven path must be untouched).

Commits: docs(core): GDMA coupled-mode contract + stale-claim sweep (verification has no separate commit).

docs.title: Self-review notes
docs.text:
• Parent-spec coverage (Slice 3 GDMA item): UART/SPI/I2S coupling (Tasks 2–4), EOF/interrupt semantics per direction (each task's tests + existing source-id wiring), LCD deferred (Task 5 doc), compatibility preserved for non-coupled peripherals (Task 1 fallback + pinned test).
  • Deliberately out: e2e cross-compiled DMA firmware fixtures (Xtensa toolchain dependency — register-level tests gate the logic; a firmware fixture is a fast-follow once a toolchain-built ELF can be committed like other fixtures); virtual netif (Slice 3B, separate plan); dual-core verification (3C).
  • The riskiest unknowns are pinned to Task 0 (PERI_SEL ground truth) and the Task 3 coupling-mechanism decision (investigate-first instruction with two named candidate patterns from the codebase).
  • No placeholder steps: every task names its test cases, files, gates, and commit message; implementation details that depend on unread code are framed as verify-then-adapt with exact anchors.

docs.title: Plan 1.5 — HW Runner Cleanup and Sim-Side TODO Closure
docs.text: Date: 2026-04-25 Branch target: feature/esp32s3-plan1.5-cleanup (off feature/esp32s3-plan1-foundation) Predecessor: Plan 1 (docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md, branch feature/esp32s3-plan1-foundation) Successor: Plan 2 — Boot Path + UART (M3) and Plan 3 — GPIO + SYSTIMER + Blinky (M4)

docs.title: 1. Goal
docs.text:
Close exit criterion 4 from Plan 1 (full 90/90 _hw/_diff variants pass on the connected ESP32-S3-Zero) and close the four documented sim-side gaps listed in docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md §"Known Gaps and Acknowledged Limitations" (MOVSP register spill, ROTW privilege check, RFWU for UF8/UF12, S32E outside-vector IllegalInstruction). Every fixed behavior gets a dedicated #[hw_oracle_test] per the §7.5 methodology rule from the ESP32-S3-Zero design spec.

The two TODO(plan2) source comments in crates/core/src/cpu/xtensa_lx7.rs (both in the MOVSP spill arm) are removed as part of the MOVSP fix. The other three sim-side gaps don't have explicit TODO(plan2) comments in code — they're documented only in the case study's Known Gaps section, and closing them means removing them from that section.

After Plan 1.5, Plan 1 reads as 100 % closed and the simulator's Xtensa LX7 core has zero deferred behaviors. Plan 2 (boot path + UART) inherits a clean foundation.

docs.title: 2. Non-goals
docs.text:
• Any peripheral work (UART, GPIO, SYSTIMER, intmatrix) — that is Plan 2 / Plan 3.
  • Any boot-path work — that is Plan 2.
  • Any new system module under crates/core/src/system/ — xtensa.rs does not exist yet, and is not added by this plan. Plan 2 introduces it.
  • Any change to the ELF flashing strategy (still Strategy C: OpenOCD program command).
  • Any changes to the four pre-existing cross-compile failures (arm-hello, firmware-ci-fixture, riscv-hello, demo_blinky) — those predate Plan 1 and remain --exclude'd.
  • Any new architecture support, decoder additions, or instruction implementations beyond what's needed to close the four sim TODOs.

docs.title: 3. Context
docs.text:
Plan 1 closed with 62/90 _hw/_diff variants passing and the headline fibonacci_10_diff bit-exact. The remaining 28 failures cluster into five root causes documented in docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md §"Live HW oracle bank — current state". All five are in the HW runner / OpenOCD interface layer; the simulator semantics for the cases that pass agree with the chip bit-for-bit.

In addition to the runner-side failures, Plan 1 deliberately shipped four TODO(plan2) markers in the simulator core covering MOVSP register spill, RFWU for UF8/UF12, ROTW privilege check, and the IllegalInstruction path for S32E outside an exception vector. These are documented in docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md §"Known Gaps and Acknowledged Limitations".

This plan delivers both surfaces in one branch. The work is largely independent across the nine fixes, so subagent-driven development with one fix per task is the natural execution pattern.

docs.title: 4.1 Three-layer split (unchanged from Plan 1)
docs.text:
This plan does not change the three-layer architecture established in Plan 1:

  • Simulator (crates/core/) — the Cpu / Bus / Peripheral abstraction.
  • Hardware oracle (crates/hw-oracle/, crates/hw-oracle-macros/) — the #[hw_oracle_test] macro and its OpenOCD-backed runtime.
  • Test fixtures (fixtures/xtensa-asm/) — hand-written Xtensa asm bytecode the oracle harness flashes and runs.

All Plan 1.5 work is internal to the first two layers.

docs.title: 4.2 HW runner extension points
docs.text:
The OpenOCD wrapper in crates/hw-oracle/src/openocd.rs gains three named capabilities, each behind a small public API surface:

  1. SR dispatch table. A static HashMap<&'static str, SrAccessForm> populated lazily on first use per session. SrAccessForm is one of XtensaRsr, OcdReg, or Reg. The runner probes each form once per SR by reading and writing back the same value via each command, accepting the form whose readback round-trips correctly.
  2. Windowed-register translation. New methods read_logical_ar(idx: u8) -> u32 and write_logical_ar(idx: u8, val: u32) that read the current WindowBase SR (using the SR dispatch table from #1), compute phys = ((wb << 2) + idx) & 0x3F, and route to physical AR access. The existing read_register("aN") and write_register("aN", v) calls dispatch through this layer when the name matches the regex ^a(0|1[0-5]|[1-9])$. Other register names (PC, PS, SR names) bypass.
  3. VECBASE write sequencer. A new set_vecbase(addr: u32) that wraps whatever halt-write-flush-resume sequence the empirical investigation finds. The current write_register("vecbase", ...) call sites switch to this method.

These three capabilities are the only API additions; everything else inside openocd.rs is internal refactoring.

docs.title: 4.3 Simulator extension points
docs.text:
Four narrow changes to crates/core/src/cpu/xtensa_lx7.rs:

  1. MOVSP exec arm gains a real spill loop before the existing AllocaCause exception entry. The spill writes physical AR slots (WB+1)*4 .. (WB+1)*4 + 4 to [a1 - 16, a1) per the windowed ABI when WindowStart[(WB+1) & 0xF] is set, then proceeds with the existing exception entry.
  2. RFWU exec arm generalises the existing UF4 path. The number of frames to reload is N/4 where N is encoded in the RFWU instruction's immediate (4, 8, or 12). Per-frame restore loop indexed by window-rotation offset.
  3. ROTW exec arm gains a if self.ps.ring() != 0 { return self.raise_general_exception(8); } guard at the top, where 8 is PrivilegedCause per the existing inline comment at the ROTW arm (// if ps.ring() != 0 { raise PrivilegedCause (EXCCAUSE=8) }).
  4. S32E/L32E disambiguation in step() changes the EXCM-gated fall-through. Currently when EXCM is unset, the encoding is treated as S32I.N. After this fix, when EXCM is unset and the encoding pattern matches S32E/L32E specifically (not other QRST encodings), the step raises IllegalInstruction.

docs.title: 4.4 Oracle test additions
docs.text:
Nine new #[hw_oracle_test] entries in crates/hw-oracle/tests/oracles.rs:

  • 4 sim-side regression tests (one per sim fix above).
  • 4 replacements for the wrong-illegal-opcode tests (root cause #4): the existing tests are removed and replaced with tests using opcodes xtensa-esp32s3-elf-as confirms are actually invalid (e.g., reserved bit-pattern 0xFFFFFF if confirmed reserved by HW probe; otherwise, an opcode the assembler rejects and which the live S3-Zero raises EXCCAUSE=0 on).
  • 1 windowed-register translation regression test that pre-loads WindowBase to a non-default value (e.g., 4), writes a3 via the runner, and asserts that physical ar15 (= (4*4 + 3) & 0x3F) holds the value.

In addition, the four sim-side fixes naturally re-enable existing _hw tests that were #[ignore]-blocked because the sim path failed; those #[ignore] attributes are removed.

docs.title: 5.1 crates/hw-oracle/src/openocd.rs
docs.text:
Addition API Lines (rough) SR access form enum + per-SR cache enum SrAccessForm, fn probe_sr_form(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<SrAccessForm>, fn read_sr(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<u32>, fn write_sr(&mut self, name: &str, val: u32) -> Result<()> ~80 Windowed AR translation fn read_logical_ar(&mut self, idx: u8) -> Result<u32>, fn write_logical_ar(&mut self, idx: u8, val: u32) -> Result<()> ~40 read_register / write_register dispatch update (changed signature, same name) ~30 VECBASE sequencer fn set_vecbase(&mut self, addr: u32) -> Result<()> ~40 Sub-word DRAM readback workaround (internal helper used by oracle tests; documented in docs/hw-oracle-runner-setup.md) ~20

Total: roughly +200 lines, ~50 lines refactored.

docs.title: 5.2 crates/core/src/cpu/xtensa_lx7.rs
docs.text:
Change Site MOVSP spill loop within the existing MOVSP arm, before the raise_general_exception(ALLOCA_CAUSE) call RFWU loop generalisation within the existing RFWU arm; replace UF4-only logic with N-frame loop ROTW privilege guard one-line guard at top of ROTW arm S32E/L32E IllegalInstruction path within the EXCM-gated disambiguation in step()

Total: roughly +150 lines, mostly the MOVSP spill loop.

docs.title: 5.3 crates/hw-oracle/tests/oracles.rs
docs.text: Nine new #[hw_oracle_test] entries. Each follows the existing pattern in the file: a closure setting up registers / memory, a closure asserting end state, and the macro emitting _sim/_hw/_diff triplets.

docs.title: 5.4 EXCCAUSE constants
docs.text: The existing code uses literal EXCCAUSE codes inline (e.g., raise_general_exception(0) for IllegalInstruction, raise_general_exception(8) for PrivilegedCause as commented at the ROTW arm). The implementation plan continues this convention rather than introducing constants — the inline literals are already documented by adjacent comments. If the implementer prefers constants, they may extract EXCCAUSE_ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION = 0 and EXCCAUSE_PRIVILEGED = 8 to crates/core/src/cpu/xtensa_sr.rs as a non-functional refactor; that decision is left to the implementer and is not load-bearing.

docs.title: 5.5 docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md
docs.text: Append an "Addendum: Plan 1.5 closeout" section noting: - Exit criterion 4 now reads PASS — 90/90. - Both TODO(plan2) source comments in cpu/xtensa_lx7.rs removed (both were in the MOVSP path). - The four sim-side gaps in the case study's "Known Gaps and Acknowledged Limitations" section all closed — that section reduced to "Pre-existing workspace cross-compile failures" only (which predate Plan 1). - New oracle test count. - Reference to the Plan 1.5 spec and implementation plan.

docs.title: 5.6 docs/hw-oracle-runner-setup.md
docs.text: Update with notes on: - Which OpenOCD command form works for each SR class (output of probe). - The windowed-register translation behaviour (developer-facing contract: "use logical aN names; the runner handles WB"). - The VECBASE write sequence the runner uses. - The sub-word DRAM readback workaround (if the empirical investigation lands on one).

docs.title: 6. Data flow
docs.text: No new data flows. The HW runner's existing flow — OracleCase constructs sim and HW state, runs both to a BREAK, diffs registers + memory + selected SRs — is unchanged. The three new HW runner capabilities (SR dispatch, windowed translation, VECBASE sequencer) are pure transparent improvements to the existing register- and SR-access paths.

docs.title: 7. Error handling
docs.text:
The HW runner's existing Result chain is preserved. Two new failure modes:

  1. SR probe fails for all forms. probe_sr_form returns an error indicating the SR is not accessible via any known OpenOCD command. Surfaces as a clear test failure with the SR name and the probed forms tried.
  2. Windowed register index out of range. read_logical_ar / write_logical_ar panic on idx >= 16 (programmer error; the AR window is always 16 wide).

Sim-side error handling for the four new fixes uses the existing raise_general_exception machinery; no new error types are added to SimulationError.

docs.title: 8.1 Per-fix rhythm
docs.text:
Every fix lands as its own commit with the new oracle test in the same commit. Sequence per fix:

  1. Write the oracle test for the new behaviour.
  2. Verify _sim FAILs (or PASSes if the test is for the runner only) the way the bug predicts.
  3. Verify _hw FAILs the way the bug predicts on the live board.
  4. Land the fix.
  5. Verify both _sim and _hw PASS and _diff is bit-exact.
  6. Re-run the full HW oracle bank to confirm the fix did not regress the previously-passing 62 cases.

docs.title: 8.2 Sim-path regression gate
docs.text:
After every commit:

cargo test --workspace --exclude firmware --exclude firmware-ci-fixture --exclude riscv-ci-fixture

must stay at 461+ green. Each new sim test increments the count by 1.

docs.title: 8.3 HW-path validation
docs.text:
The live S3-Zero is connected for this work. After each fix:

cargo test -p labwired-hw-oracle --features hw-oracle -- --ignored --test-threads=1

The full HW bank runs end-of-task to confirm no regression.

docs.title: 8.4 Sequencing
docs.text:
Tasks are largely independent. Recommended order:

Order Fix Why this slot 1 HW root cause #1 (SR name dispatch) Unblocks tests gated on epc1 and other SR readback. Several other tests depend on this working. 2 HW root cause #2 (windowed-reg translation) Unblocks the largest single batch of failing tests (~10). 3 HW root cause #3 (VECBASE sequencer) Smaller batch (3 tests) but conceptually clean. 4 HW root cause #4 (illegal-opcode replacements) Mechanical; depends on ad-hoc probing of which opcodes are actually invalid. 5 HW root cause #5 (sub-word DRAM readback) The murky one; gets last so easy wins are banked first. Time-box: half a day. 6 Sim ROTW privilege check One-line; warm-up. 7 Sim S32E IllegalInstruction Small; clear. 8 Sim RFWU UF8/UF12 Generalises existing UF4 logic. 9 Sim MOVSP spill Largest; gets last so RFWU's frame-walking helpers can be reused.

docs.title: 9. Exit criteria
docs.text: # Criterion Verification 1 Sim suite stays green cargo test --workspace --exclude firmware --exclude firmware-ci-fixture --exclude riscv-ci-fixture reports ≥ 470 passing (461 baseline + at least 9 new sim tests; some fixes add more than one) 2 All HW oracle variants pass cargo test -p labwired-hw-oracle --features hw-oracle -- --ignored --test-threads=1 reports 90/90 (or higher if new tests added cleanly) 3 Both TODO(plan2) source comments removed and four documented sim-side gaps closed grep -c "TODO(plan2)" crates/core/src/cpu/xtensa_lx7.rs returns 0; docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md "Known Gaps" section retains only the pre-existing cross-compile entry 4 Each new behaviour has a dedicated oracle test grep -c "#\[hw_oracle_test\]" crates/hw-oracle/tests/oracles.rs increases by ≥ 9 from baseline 5 Case study addendum reflects 90/90 + closed sim TODOs docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md ends with an "Addendum: Plan 1.5 closeout" section 6 No new #[ignore] markers added grep -c "#\[ignore\]" crates/hw-oracle/tests/oracles.rs ≤ baseline 7 Runner setup doc updated docs/hw-oracle-runner-setup.md mentions SR dispatch, windowed translation, VECBASE sequencer, sub-word DRAM workaround

docs.title: 10. Risks and mitigations
docs.text: Risk Likelihood Impact Mitigation HW root cause #5 (sub-word DRAM readback) won't yield to the obvious fixes Medium Medium — could blow the time budget Time-box to half a day. Fallback workaround: have the test fixture's load instruction be followed by or aT, aT, a0 so the writeback is reified through a normal AR write; document the workaround in docs/hw-oracle-runner-setup.md and accept it as the chosen behaviour. MOVSP spill semantics diverge from real silicon in a corner case Medium Medium — could cause a _diff mismatch on a complex spill scenario Cross-validate against ESP-IDF xtensa-rt's _WindowSpill macro (MIT-licensed, allowed per design doc §7.6 reference rule). Add a multi-frame spill oracle test as part of the MOVSP fix; bit-exact diff is the verifier. OpenOCD SR probe sequence triggers a target halt-state issue (e.g., one of the tried command forms leaves the target in a bad state) Low Medium — could break the session for subsequent tests Probe inside a halt context that's known-safe; on probe failure for a given form, issue an explicit reset halt before trying the next form. Cache the working form so probes happen at most once per session per SR. Windowed-register translation introduces a subtle off-by-one in physical-slot computation Low High — every windowed test silently writes to the wrong slot The dedicated translation regression test (Section 4.4 last bullet) catches this directly. Sim and HW paths both compute the physical slot independently; the _diff check verifies they agree. Replacement illegal opcodes turn out to also be valid on this LX7 variant Low Low — just probe more opcodes Use xtensa-esp32s3-elf-as as the first authority: any opcode the assembler refuses to emit is a candidate. Then probe on the live board (write, single-step, expect EXCCAUSE=0) before committing to it.

docs.title: 11. Schedule
docs.text:
Estimated effort: 3–4 working days for the implementer.

Day Tasks Day 1 HW root causes 1, 2, 3 (SR dispatch, windowed translation, VECBASE) Day 2 HW root causes 4, 5 (illegal-opcode replacements, sub-word DRAM) + Sim ROTW + Sim S32E Day 3 Sim RFWU 8/12, Sim MOVSP spill (largest single fix) Day 4 Case study addendum, runner-setup doc updates, full HW bank verification, branch finalisation

docs.title: 12. References
docs.text:
• docs/design/2026-04-24-esp32s3-zero-digital-twin-design.md (the design spec)
  • docs/case_study_esp32s3_plan1.md (Plan 1 closeout, including the documented gaps this plan closes)
  • docs/implementation/2026-04-24-esp32s3-plan-1-foundation.md (Plan 1 implementation plan)
  • ESP-IDF xtensa-rt _WindowSpill macro — MIT-licensed reference for MOVSP spill semantics
  • Cadence Xtensa ISA Reference Manual — first authority for instruction semantics
  • Real ESP32-S3-Zero via OpenOCD — first authority for behavioural ground truth (per design doc §7.6)

docs.title: LabWired Core v0.14.0 Release Prep Design
Goal
docs.text: Prepare and ship LabWired Core v0.14.0 as a minor release that captures the substantial hardware-validation, board-support, simulator, and documentation work completed after v0.13.0.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: This release prep updates release metadata and release-facing documentation only. It does not change simulator behavior, delete tracked fixtures, upgrade dependencies for their own sake, or rewrite generated catalog data.

docs.title: Versioning
docs.text: 0.14.0 is a minor bump because the post-v0.13.0 history includes new capabilities: ESP32-S3 support, STM32L476 and STM32F407 hardware-validation lanes, expanded peripherals, hardware-oracle tooling, and DAP/debugger improvements.

docs.title: Documentation Updates
docs.text:
• Cargo.toml workspace package version moves from 0.13.0 to 0.14.0.
  • Cargo.lock is refreshed by Cargo after the version change.
  • CHANGELOG.md gets a dated 0.14.0 section, a new empty Unreleased section, and concise release notes based on commits since v0.13.0.
  • README.md install snippets pin LABWIRED_VERSION=v0.14.0.
  • RELEASE_PROCESS.md replaces stale concrete v0.12.0 examples with neutral vX.Y.Z examples.
  • RELEASE_READINESS_CHECKLIST.md becomes a fresh v0.14.0 checklist rather than a completed v0.13.0 artifact.
  • ROADMAP.md and scripts/install.sh stop presenting old versions as current examples.

docs.title: Cleanup
docs.text: Tracked generated outputs under out/**, examples/nucleo-h563zi/target/firmware, examples/arm-c-hello/src/main.o, and the zero-byte examples/hil-displacement-showcase/hil_displacement_showcase.bin are removed from source control. Intentional test fixtures under tests/fixtures, crates/core/tests/fixtures, fixtures/xtensa-asm, and golden-reference directories are preserved.

docs.title: Release Publication
docs.text: After verification passes, commit the release prep changes, create an annotated v0.14.0 tag, push main and the tag, and create a GitHub release using the CHANGELOG.md 0.14.0 notes.

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
Run:

  • cargo check
  • cargo fmt --all -- --check
  • cargo test --workspace
  • rg -n 'v0\\.13\\.0|0\\.13\\.0|v0\\.12\\.0|0\\.12\\.0' README.md RELEASE_PROCESS.md RELEASE_READINESS_CHECKLIST.md ROADMAP.md scripts/install.sh Cargo.toml CHANGELOG.md

The final grep may still show historical changelog entries for old releases. Release-facing current instructions must not point to stale versions.

docs.title: ESP32-S3 Full Chip Model — Design
docs.text: Date: 2026-06-05 Status: Draft for review Scope: ESP32-S3 (Xtensa LX7) only. Classic ESP32 / generic Xtensa / ARM / RISC-V paths are out of scope and untouched.

docs.title: 1. Goal & definition of done
docs.text:
Make the ESP32-S3 a faithful-by-default, register-complete chip model:

  1. Faithful by default. The real-ROM path runs automatically with no env-var dance. The thunk path is retained only as an explicit, clearly-labelled graceful fallback when the ROM blob cannot be located.
  2. Register-complete + state-machine-faithful (primary objective). Every peripheral models every register and every field with correct access semantics (RW / RO / W1C / W1S / self-clearing / read-as-zero), correct reset values, and all of its documented internal state machines — not merely the subset that the firmware exercised so far.

"0-thunk boot of real firmware" is necessary but not sufficient. A peripheral is only "done" when its full register map and FSMs are modelled and validated.

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text:
• Radio PHY/MAC (WiFi, BLE) — explicitly out of scope.
  • Register-completeness for the classic ESP32 or other architectures.
  • Cycle-exact timing (functional FSM fidelity, not pipeline timing).

docs.title: 2. The completeness bar (per-peripheral definition of done)
docs.text:
A peripheral meets the bar when all of the following hold, tracked in the coverage matrix (§4):

  1. Register map complete. Every register and field present in the ESP32-S3 SVD (§3 oracle) is modelled: correct offset, width, reset value, and access type. No silent "read-as-zero / accept-and-ignore" for registers a real driver reads or writes.
  2. Access semantics correct. W1C (write-1-to-clear), W1S, self-clearing trigger bits (e.g. CONF_UPGATE, FIFO-reset, TRANS_START), RO status fields, and latched fields behave as silicon.
  3. Internal state machine(s) faithful. The peripheral's documented FSMs are implemented and observable through the registers — e.g. I2C command-list engine, SPI master transaction FSM, SAR-ADC conversion FSM, RMT TX/RX encoder, LEDC/MCPWM counter+compare, UART FIFO occupancy, timer counter/alarm.
  4. Interrupts complete. Every INT_RAW/INT_ENA/INT_ST/INT_CLR bit is modelled with correct set/clear behaviour, and the peripheral asserts the correct interrupt-matrix source ID (the ets_isr_source_t ordinal — the class of bug fixed for I2C0: source 42, not 49).
  5. Validated three ways: SVD register-coverage diff is green (§4); register-level unit tests cover the FSM transitions and access semantics; and a real ESP-IDF/Arduino driver drives it to success over the faithful 0-thunk --rom-boot path. HW-oracle trace where a physical board is on hand.

docs.title: 3. Completeness oracle: the ESP32-S3 SVD + PAC
docs.text:
Authoritative register sources, already present:

  • ~/.platformio/platforms/espressif32/misc/svd/esp32s3.svd — every peripheral, register, field, reset value, access type.
  • esp32s3 PAC (crates.io 0.35.2) — cross-check for field semantics.
  • Repo already ships crates/svd-ingestor/ (SVD → register-map parser).

The model is measured against the SVD, so "all registers modelled" is an automatable check, not a judgement call.

docs.title: 4. Coverage tooling (the tracking artifact)
docs.text:
New dev tool: xtask svd-coverage --chip esp32s3 (or a cargo test lane):

  • Parse the SVD into the authoritative {peripheral → registers → fields} set.
  • Introspect each modelled peripheral's known offsets/fields (via a small ModelledRegisters descriptor each peripheral exposes, or by parsing its REG_* constants + a declared field table).
  • Emit a coverage matrix: per peripheral, modelled / total registers and fields, with a list of unmodelled or semantically-divergent registers.
  • A ratchet: the matrix is committed; CI fails if coverage regresses. Peripherals graduate to "complete" when 100% with FSM tests attached.

This matrix is the living definition of "full chip model" — the chip is full when every in-scope peripheral row is green.

docs.title: 4.1 Shipped (2026-06-05) — labwired coverage
docs.text:
Implemented as a CLI subcommand (not an xtask), since it reuses the wired bus:

cargo run -p labwired-cli -- coverage [--svd <path>] [--json-out <path>]

The introspection method (user decision) is a behavioral probe, not a declared table — so the number cannot be self-graded. The pure engine lives in crates/core/src/coverage/probe.rs; the SVD/bus driver in crates/cli/src/coverage.rs. For each SVD register the probe drives the wired model and classifies:

  • Modelled — the register retains a written sentinel (two complementary patterns catch any writable bit) OR reads a value distinct from the peripheral's own unmapped-offset catch-all baseline.
  • Unmodelled — a read-write register that behaves exactly like an unmapped offset (write ignored, read == catch-all): an accept-and-ignore stub.
  • Indeterminate — cannot be decided by probing: a write-only/read-only register reading the catch-all value, or a peripheral whose catch-all itself round-trips arbitrary writes (generic HashMap/overflow-backed storage — it scores Indeterminate, NEVER Modelled; this is the anti-gaming guarantee).

Authoritative SVD discovered from the toolchain (LABWIRED_ESP32S3_SVD override, else the PlatformIO espressif32 path); not vendored (Espressif-licensed). Snapshot committed at docs/coverage/esp32s3-coverage.json; the gated ratchet test crates/cli/tests/svd_coverage_ratchet.rs fails if any peripheral's modelled count drops below it.

Baseline (2026-06-05): ~1995 Modelled / 1008 Indeterminate / 1192 Unmodelled of 4195 SVD registers across 54 peripherals. I2C0 = 14/0/22 of 36 (the 22 unmodelled are the SCL-timing/config registers the model accept-and-ignores).

Known probe limitations (resolved by the per-peripheral FSM tests in S4+, not by changing the probe): reactive read-only status registers that read 0 when idle (e.g. I2C0 SR) score Unmodelled because they're indistinguishable from a stub without a live transaction; write-only trigger registers with no read-back side effect score Indeterminate; peripherals backed by generic round-trip storage (SHA, MCPWM) score all-Indeterminate even where real FSMs exist. The probe drives real model behavior, so a model that panics on a sentinel write must be fixed in the model (there is no catch_unwind net — the workspace builds panic=abort).

docs.title: 5.1 ROM auto-provisioning (boot::esp32s3::rom_provision)
docs.text:
At configure_xtensa_esp32s3, if no explicit LABWIRED_ESP32S3_ROM override: 1. Discover the toolchain ROM ELF (search ~/.platformio/tools/tool-esp-rom-elfs/esp32s3_rev0_rom.elf, $IDF_PATH, an env hint). 2. Extract flat IROM (0x4000_0000, 384 KiB) and DROM (0x3FF0_0000, 128 KiB) images in Rust — a port of scripts/make_esp32s3_rom_bins.py, including the .data copy-source reconstruction and p_paddr-based IROM layout (the working extraction does more than a naive PT_LOAD-by-vaddr copy). 3. Cache under a state dir, keyed on the ELF content hash. 4. Load as today. The ROM blob is never vendored (Espressif copyright); it is read from the user's own installed toolchain.

If discovery fails → fall back to the thunk harness with a one-line notice: "ESP32-S3 ROM not found; running in degraded harness mode — install the ESP toolchain for faithful simulation."

docs.title: 5.2 Faithful-default switch + cleanup
docs.text:
• configure_xtensa_esp32s3 defaults to the faithful path when the blob resolves; thunk harness retained as fallback (shared RomThunkBank, untouched for classic ESP32).
  • ~~Delete wifi_thunks.rs~~ CORRECTION (2026-06-05): wifi_thunks.rs is NOT dead code — it is live ESP32-classic WiFi bring-up (SimNet + lwIP socket thunks) exercised by crates/core/tests/e2e_labwired_wifi.rs. The earlier "dead code" claim came from a src/-only grep that missed the test. It is a separate, out-of-scope WiFi workstream and is left untouched.

docs.title: 5.3 Faithful-mode gate + telemetry
docs.text:
• The model reports its boot mode (faithful | harness) and a count of BREAK 1,14 thunk dispatches.
  • Test helper assert_faithful_zero_thunks() — real ROM loaded, 0 thunk dispatches — used by every per-peripheral boot test.

docs.title: 5.4 Per-peripheral register+FSM model (the bulk of the work)
docs.text: Each peripheral slice brings one peripheral to the §2 bar. A shared pattern: a documented register/field table (checked by the coverage tool), the FSM(s) in tick()/write_u32, complete interrupt handling, and register-level tests.

docs.title: 6. Slices / sequence
docs.text:
Infrastructure (enables everything): - S1 — ROM auto-provisioning (§5.1). - S2 — Faithful default + delete wifi_thunks (§5.2). - S3 — Coverage tool + faithful-mode gate (§4, §5.3): SVD coverage matrix committed; per-peripheral boot-test harness in place.

Per-peripheral completeness (one slice each, independently shippable): Ordered by SpiceDispenser relevance, then breadth. Each = bring the peripheral to the §2 bar (all registers + FSMs + interrupts + 3-way validation): - S4 — I2C0/I2C1: already 0-thunk boots; finish register/field completeness + FSM tests to 100% (close the remaining accept-and-ignore regs). - S5 — GP-SPI2/SPI3 (drives e-paper SSD16xx; SpiceDispenser-adjacent). - S6 — LEDC + MCPWM (servo/PWM). - S7 — SAR-ADC. - S8 — RMT. - S9 — Timer Group + SYSTIMER (audit to completeness; partially modelled). - S10 — UART/USB-Serial-JTAG. - S11+ — remaining TRM peripherals (I2S, TWAI, PCNT, SDMMC, GDMA, crypto…) driven by the coverage matrix until every in-scope row is green.

The peripheral set beyond S4–S8 is driven by the coverage matrix, not guesswork.

docs.title: 7. Testing & validation strategy
docs.text:
• SVD coverage diff (CI ratchet) — register/field completeness.
  • Register-level unit tests — FSM transitions + access semantics per peripheral (extend the existing #[cfg(test)] blocks).
  • Faithful 0-thunk boot tests — real ESP32-S3 firmware per peripheral over --rom-boot, asserting driver success + assert_faithful_zero_thunks(). Gated on the toolchain ROM ELF being present (like existing e2e firmware tests).
  • HW oracle — register-trace diff vs a physical ESP32-S3 over USB-JTAG where available (the existing oracle lane).

docs.title: 8. Risks & open questions
docs.text:
• Register introspection mechanism for the coverage tool: each peripheral must expose its modelled register/field set. Cheapest is a declared table per peripheral; needs a small trait. (Decide in S3.)
  • Firmware availability for breadth peripherals — need real S3 sketches exercising SPI/ADC/RMT/PWM. Existing examples/esp32s3-* cover some; others may need a small Arduino/IDF sketch each.
  • Effort — full register completeness across ~30 peripherals is large; the coverage matrix makes progress legible and lets us ship peripheral-by-peripheral.
  • FSM depth ceiling — functional fidelity, not cycle-exact timing (non-goal).

docs.title: Pending Silicon Verification
docs.text:
Standing rule: every chip-model fix is provisional until verified against real hardware. A sim-consistent green in the Tier-1 matrix proves the model is internally consistent with the fixture; only silicon breaks the circularity. Each model-behavior change adds an entry here in the same PR; an entry closes when its hardware verification lands (and the matrix cell graduates to the silicon-anchored tier with the HIL workstream).

# Model change PR/commit HW verification recipe Board Status 1 Bit-band translation gated on core (M3/M4 only); H5/WBA GPIO un-shadowed ee1133c New h563_mmio_diff oracle (pattern: l476_mmio_diff): GPIO word-writes at 0x4202_xxxx — ODR/BSRR/BRR data path on PB0/PF4/PG4 incl. one-word BS-over-BR priority, plus a 4-pattern parity sweep over the GPIOE/GPIOF config surface — sim vs silicon over the AP1 dapdirect recipe (OpenOcd::spawn_stm32h563). 8/8 cases + 48/48 register-patterns byte-exact under H563_STRICT=1. NUCLEO-H563ZI ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 2 T1 shift-immediate flags suppressed inside IT blocks 60445bd Instruction-level oracle: IT-block sequences with T1 LSL/LSR/ASR, APSR captured on silicon (extend thumb_oracles) STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 — it_block_shift_preserves_flags passes hw+diff on the bench F103 (PR #191) 3 Thumb-1 STRH/LDRSB/LDRH/LDRSH register-offset decode 4ebed86 Same thumb_oracles extension: loaded/stored values + sign-extension vs silicon STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 — strh_ldrh_reg_offset / ldrsb / ldrsh pass hw+diff (PR #191) 4 GDMA descriptor-walk mem-to-mem (ESP32-S3) fa292bd JTAG Unity run on the bench S3: same descriptor sequence on silicon, byte-compare (recipe: HW_ORACLE_RESULT.md in the platformio demo) bench ESP32-S3 (proven setup) open 5 ESP32-C3 TIMG0 wired to the real Timg model 9dfe444 T0 counter advance + latch semantics on silicon (JTAG or UART-reporting probe firmware) ESP32-C3 board availability unconfirmed — blocked on hardware until then open (blocked-on-HW) 6 RV32C compressed-branch decode fix drifted the riscv_uart_ok trace fingerprint c7148f2 Corrected diagnosis. The nightly Trace Drift Assertions red was not demo-blinky pacing — the four trace cases never load blinky firmware. The gate (scripts/trace_drift_assert.sh) is a sim-regression snapshot: it fingerprints sim result+snapshot+UART against committed examples/ci/fingerprints/, not a silicon diff. The RVC decoder fix (#7) landed after the baseline (#76) → riscv_uart_ok legitimately drifted; the firmware still prints correct RV OK and passes. Re-baselined riscv_uart_ok.sha256 (the only drifting case; the three ARM cases still match). n/a (sim-only gate) resolved (re-baselined) 7 RV32C compressed-branch offset encoding (C.BEQZ/C.BNEZ/C.J) + funct3=4 arithmetic group c7148f2 Run the same RV32C branch/jump sequence on real RISC-V silicon (ESP32-C3 RV32IMC) and compare taken/not-taken outcomes + target PCs against the model ESP32-C3 (board availability unconfirmed — shares #5's blocker) open (blocked-on-HW) 8 Cortex-M shift family (LSL/LSR/ASR/ROR) sets the carry flag — immediate and register forms (was N/Z only); register forms also now honour IT-block flag suppression PR #191 Caught BY silicon, not pending it. Adding xpsr/NZCV diffing to thumb_oracles failed lsrs_immediate against the bench F103 (sim C=0 vs silicon C=1). Fixed the immediate forms, then the register forms (LslReg/LsrReg/AsrReg), then Ror — all three carried the same gap. ADDS/SUBS carry+overflow flags also silicon-anchored. Re-verified — 54/54 hw+diff on the F103. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 9 F103 WDT (IWDG/WWDG) + USART2 + DMA1 + RTC (CRH/CNT) register reset values PR #192 Reset values probed off the bench F103 via OpenOCD, pinned as stm32f1_mmio_diff RESET_CASES; sim matches silicon. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 10 F103 was wired to the L4 calendar-RTC model, not an F1 counter RTC PR #192 Root cause of the CRL=0x2101 discrepancy: the F1 RTC (CRH/CRL/PRL/CNT counter) is a different peripheral from the L4 calendar RTC (TR/DR/CR/ISR); CRL@0x04 was reading the L4 DR reset (0x2101). New rtc_f1 model + F103 yaml rewire; CRH/CNT now match silicon for the right reason. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ fixed 2026-06-08 (model) — remaining: cold CRL=0 vs idle 0x20 needs a clock-aware RTC to pin across the cold→synced transition 11 TIM2 (general-purpose timer) update-generation event latches the CCxIF compare-match flags, not UIF alone PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the new peripheral-execution oracle. stm32f1_exec_oracle runs real ARM code on a full F103 chip bus (sim) and on silicon: enable TIM2 clock, set PSC/ARR, seed CNT non-zero, write EGR.UG, read back. Sim said SR=0x01 (UIF only); silicon said SR=0x1F — the UG reload sets CNT=0, which matches every reset-zero CCRx in output-compare mode → CC1..4IF also latch (documented STM32 gotcha). Model now latches compare-match on UG (!basic); re-verified hw+diff byte-exact on the bench F103. CNT/ARR/PSC also cross-checked. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 11a Basic timers (TIM6/TIM7) gated out of the UG compare-match path (basic: true) on STM32L073/L476 PR (exec-oracle) Config-correct per RM0008 (basic timers have no capture/compare channels, so UG sets UIF only) but not yet silicon-verified — no L0/L4 board on the bench. Verify with an L0/L4 over SWD: enable TIM6 clock, EGR.UG, read SR → expect 0x01. STM32L0/L4 (none on bench) open (config-correct, HW-pending) 12 GPIO 32-bit word writes go through an atomic write_u32 (was byte-decomposed) so BSRR honours BS-over-BR priority PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle. gpioa_bsrr_set_reset writes BSRR=0x0010_0010 (set pin4 AND reset pin4): silicon's single 32-bit transaction applies BS priority → ODR pin4 set; sim's default byte-decomposition split the set-half (low 16) from the reset-half (high 16) into separate write_reg calls, so reset clobbered set → pin4 cleared (sim ODR=0x000F vs hw 0x001F). Added GpioPort::{read_u32,write_u32} handing the whole word to write_reg. Re-verified hw+diff byte-exact (final ODR=0x001C across BSRR-set/reset + BS-priority + F1 BRR). STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 13 F1 RCC_AHBENR reset value 0x14 (SRAMEN + FLITFEN enabled out of reset), was 0 PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle. crc32_two_words RMW-enables CRCEN; the diff flagged the scratch register holding the AHBENR read-back (sim 0x40 vs hw 0x54 = 0x14 | CRCEN). RM0008 §7.3.6: AHBENR reset 0x00000014. Set F1Rcc::new().ahbenr = 0x14; re-verified hw+diff. (The read-modify-write enable path matters — overwriting AHBENR would clear SRAMEN/FLITFEN and hang a program running from SRAM.) STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 14 DMA1 memory-to-memory engine — no model change, validated against silicon PR (exec-oracle) First oracle to drive an autonomous engine: dma1_mem_to_mem arms an 8-bit mem-to-mem copy (CMAR→CPAR, MINC/PINC, MEM2MEM|EN) then stops; a new sim-only harness knob (ThumbOracleCase::settle_ticks) ticks the bus after the program so the DMA completes (one byte/tick), mirroring silicon's concurrent run. Destination bytes, CNDTR1=0, and ISR=0x7 (GIF1|TCIF1|HTIF1) match byte-exact on the bench F103 — the existing DMA model was already correct, now silicon-anchored. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 15 EXTI 32-bit word writes go through an atomic write_u32 (was byte-decomposed) so SWIER edge-detect + PR rc_w1 behave PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle (on the sim side, pre-silicon). exti_swier_sets_and_clears_pr word-writes SWIER=0x5 then PR=0x1; the default byte-decomposition read the rc_w1 PR back and re-wrote it, clearing the still-pending line 2 (sim PR=0 vs expected 0x4). Same class as the GPIO BSRR fix (#12). Added Exti::{read_u32,write_u32}. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 16 Clearing an EXTI PR bit (rc_w1) also clears the matching SWIER bit PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle, silicon-checked before fixing. After the write_u32 fix, sim PR matched but SWIER read 0x5 vs silicon 0x4; the bench F103 _hw run confirmed silicon de-asserts the software-event line when PR is cleared (RM0008 §10.3.6). Model now clears swier &= !cleared in the PR write. Re-verified hw+diff byte-exact. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 17 AFIO_MAPR masks reserved bits to 0 on read (was stored verbatim) PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle. afio_mapr_reserved_bits_read_zero writes reserved bits 27 + 21 plus a remap bit; silicon reads the reserved bits back as 0 (_hw confirmed), the model returned them set. MAPR implements remap bits [20:0]; [23:21]/[31:27] reserved, SWJ_CFG [26:24] write-only/read-undefined. Masked the store to value & 0x001F_FFFF. (The oracle deliberately writes 0 to SWJ_CFG — 0b111 there disables SWD and would drop the debugger.) Re-verified hw+diff byte-exact. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 18 IWDG PR/RLR are write-protected until KR receives the 0x5555 unlock (was stored unconditionally) PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle. iwdg_pr_rlr_write_protected_without_key writes PR/RLR with no prior unlock; silicon keeps them at reset (PR=0, RLR=0xFFF — _hw confirmed), the model stored the writes. Model now gates PR/RLR on a write_access flag set by KR=0x5555 and cleared by any other KR code. Note: on real silicon the positive path (latch after unlock) additionally needs the IWDG clock domain running (LSI/started) — not asserted here since the 0xCCCC start key would arm a watchdog reset; the model stores on unlock alone (pragmatic for survival sims). STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 (negative path) 19 CRC_IDR is an 8-bit register on STM32F1/F0/L0 (was treated as 32-bit) PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle. crc_idr_is_8bit_on_f1 writes 0x12345678 to CRC_IDR; silicon keeps only the low byte (0x78 — _hw confirmed), the model stored the full word. Added a with_idr_width flag on the CRC model (default 32-bit for F2+/L4+) + config: { idr_width: 8 } on the F103 and L073 yamls. F103 silicon-verified; L073 (L0) is config-correct per RM0367 §14, HW-pending. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-08 (F103; L0 HW-pending) 20 AFIO EXTICR1..4 mask their reserved upper half [31:16] to 0 (were stored verbatim) PR (exec-oracle) Caught BY the oracle. afio_exticr1_upper_half_reads_zero sets a reserved upper bit + a valid nibble; silicon reads the upper half back as 0 (_hw confirmed). Each EXTICR holds four 4-bit fields in [15:0] (RM0008 §9.4.3); masked the four stores to value & 0xFFFF. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-09 21 GPIO CRL/CRH round-trip + DBGMCU_CR round-trip — no model change, validated against silicon PR (exec-oracle) Two validation oracles, both byte-exact sim-vs-silicon. gpioa_crl_crh_round_trip confirms the 32-bit config registers store verbatim (no reserved fields). dbgmcu_cr_round_trip is a silicon surprise: RM0008 §31.16.2 marks DBGMCU_CR bits [4:3] reserved, but the bench F103 reads them back as written (0x1F, not 0x07) — they are plain R/W storage here. The model's verbatim store already matches; the oracle pins the real behaviour so a future "mask the reserved bits" change can't silently break it. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-09 22 NVIC ISER/ICER set-enable / clear-enable — no model change, validated against silicon PR (exec-oracle) nvic_iser_icer_enable_disable enables IRQ6+IRQ28 via ISER0 then clear-enables IRQ6 via ICER0; ISER0 reads back with IRQ28 still set (_hw byte-exact). Wires the Cortex-M block (NVIC @ 0xE000E100, SCB, DWT) into the exec-oracle bus via configure_cortex_m, validating the interrupt-enable machinery and exposing DWT_CYCCNT for future timed oracles. Interrupt delivery (exception entry/exit) is the next step — see roadmap.md P1. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-09 23 Interrupt delivery — full exception entry/exit path validated against silicon (roadmap P1) PR (exec-oracle) exti0_interrupt_delivery: executed code relocates VTOR to a [vector table][ISR][main] program, unmasks EXTI line 0, enables IRQ6 in the NVIC, clears PRIMASK, and software-triggers the line. The CPU vectors to the ISR (entry + register stacking), which sets a RAM marker + clears EXTI_PR, then BX LR returns (unstacking) to main. Marker set + EXTI_PR cleared, byte-exact on the bench F103 (_hw/_diff). Harness additions: Thumb::Data (vector words), bx/cpsie_i encoders, per-case entry_offset, and opt-in live_peripherals (post-step tick_peripherals_fully → set_exception_pending). No model bug — the NVIC/SCB/exception path was already correct; now anchored. Limitation: sim level-source re-pend is tick-granular, so non-idempotent ISRs may run an extra time vs silicon — fine for the marker ISR. STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-09 24 DWT cycle counter mechanism (enable → count → read) — no model change, validated against silicon (roadmap P2) PR (exec-oracle) dwt_cyccnt_advances enables DEMCR.TRCENA + DWT_CTRL.CYCCNTENA, resets CYCCNT, reads it back and records the self-relative boolean CYCCNT != 0. The absolute count diverges (sim is not cycle-accurate — its DWT advances one tick per executed instruction; silicon counts real core cycles), so only the boolean is invariant and the divergent register is cleared before the snapshot. Pins the DWT timing mechanism, not cycle fidelity (out of scope for this sim). STM32F103 (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-09 25 Firmware-level conformance differential (roadmap P5 v1) — found peripheral access/latch latency gaps PR (conformance) firmware-f103-conformance drives every peripheral and writes a digest; f103_conformance.rs runs the same ELF on the full-chip Machine and on silicon and diffs. First run 10/11 (91%). Found gaps the micro-oracles can't: CRC reset latency (feeding DR right after CR.RESET lost a word on silicon) and TIM2 status-latch latency (SR read 0 one instruction after EGR.UG). Correct firmware (a settle() between the hazardous accesses) fixes both; the sim is lenient because it models no peripheral access/latch latency. Residual exti_pr is a firmware-context HW artifact (the isolated EXTI oracle confirms the sim value is correct). STM32F103 (bench) ✅ pipeline silicon-proven 2026-06-09 (10/11; latency-modeling is a documented sim limitation) 26 STM32H5 SPI v3 layout (stm32h5 profile): CFG1 reserved mask, SPE config-lock, mode-fault machinery (MASTER refused + MODF latch on SS-low), CTSIZE mirror, TXTF/IFCR, TXC restore h563-class-models Verified at birth — every behavior was bench-probed BEFORE modeling (capture corpus probe-20260611), then re-proven by h563_class_diff (14 SPI cases byte-exact, H563_STRICT=1). Sim TX engine (EOT on TSIZE exhaustion) is spec-derived: the bench part had no spi_ker_ck, so real frame movement is yaml-documented as a divergence. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 (register surface; data path sim-only by documented divergence) 27 ADC L4-layout power-up handshake: ADEN with ADVREGEN up (DEEPPWD clear) raises ISR.ADRDY h563-class-models h563_class_diff ADC sequence (DEEPPWD reset → clear → ADVREGEN → ADEN→ADRDY) byte-exact vs the bench H563. The same model serves L476 — its L4 silicon re-check rides the existing L476 oracle backlog. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 (H563; L476 cross-check pending) 28 RTC v3 model (rtc_v3): WPR key gate, INIT→INITF, BYPSHAD, INITS-from-year, calendar BCD tick, locked-write drop h563-class-models Bench-probed before modeling (incl. the BYPSHAD=0 shadow-stale surprise, kept as a documented simplification); re-proven by h563_class_diff (9 RTC cases byte-exact) — including the silicon-sticky RTCSEL write-once note. 1 Hz tick approximated at 32768 sim ticks/second. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 29 GPDMA model (8ch, software-request mem-to-mem): TCF/HTF/IDLEF, BNDT drain, SAR/DAR advance, EN auto-clear, CFCR w1c, RESET-does-not-clear-flags h563-class-models The autonomous engine was driven ON SILICON over SWD (real SRAM→SRAM block copy) and the same flow is replayed by h563_class_diff (8 cases incl. destination words byte-exact). Linked-list mode + peripheral-request mode not modeled (documented). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 30 Advanced timers: CC5IF/CC6IF (SR bits 16/17) latch with the channel-1..4 compare path; compare-match also latches while COUNTING (was UG-only) h563-class-models Bench TIM1 PWM run read SR=0x0003001F (UIF+CC1..4IF+CC5/6IF) — the run-time compare and the internal channels 5/6 were both missing from the model. h563_class_diff TIM1 sequence byte-exact. F103-family UG semantics unchanged (existing oracles still green). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 31 H563 irq class: NVIC declared in the chip yaml; fixture delivery round-trip h563-class-models NVIC ISER/ICER enable machinery diffed on the M33 (h563_class_diff, 3 cases); full exception entry/exit was already silicon-anchored on the bench F103 (#23). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 (enable surface; delivery anchored via #23) 32 Timer: IRQ level follows CC1..4IF&CC1..4IE (was UIF&UIE only); wrap also latches compare flags foreign-fw twin Caught by foreign firmware (embassy time driver arms CCR1 alarms with CC1IE). Bench TIM2 probe: CC1IF + CC1IE pends the NVIC line (ISPR1 bit 13) with the CPU halted; capture7 pins the same on TIM12, embassy's actual time-driver pick. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 33 PWR H5 layout (PwrH5): VOSCR/VOSSR voltage scaling, DBPCR, register surface foreign-fw twin Bench probe capture5: resets (PMCR=0x0C, VOSSR=0x2008, SCCR=0x100, VMSR bit 20) + VOS transitions Scale3→0→2→3 read VOSSR 0x2008/0xE008/0x6008/0x2008 — model formula (VOS<<14)|ACTVOSRDY|VOSRDY is the silicon behavior. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 34 FLASH H5 layout: ACR latency/WRHIGHFREQ/PRFTEN read-back; interface regs only foreign-fw twin Bench probe capture6: ACR reset 0x13, round-trips 0x11/0x23/0x02 verbatim; OPTCR=1, NSCR=1, OPTSR_CUR pinned as representative. Program/erase not modeled (documented). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 35 TIM12 wired (embassy time-driver-any pick) foreign-fw twin Bench probe capture7: resets (ARR=0xFFFF, rest 0), PSC/CCR1/DIER round-trips, free-running CNT under the debugger. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 36 v2-USART TXEIE mask bug: was bit 3 (TE!) — every TX-enabled v2 UART held a phantom interrupt foreign-fw twin Caught by foreign firmware (embassy enables the USART3 NVIC line; the phantom pend looped in its default handler — invisible to all polling fixtures). Bench probe capture8: CR1=FIFOEN|TE|RE|UE pends nothing; +TXEIE (bit 7) pends ISPR1 bit 28. Mask fixed to bit 7 + regression test. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 37 Cortex-M: LDAEX/STLEX/LDA/STL (+B variants) implemented; CLREX→no-op; pending-exception bitmask widened 64→256 (IRQ 120 = exception 136 was silently dropped) foreign-fw twin Caught by foreign firmware: Rust atomics on thumbv8m compile to acquire/release forms the decoder routed to a silent skip — embassy's executor run-queue never woke; then its TIM12 IRQ (120) overflowed the u64 pending mask. End-to-end behavioral anchor: the same unmodified embassy ELF (usart_vcp) now produces a byte-identical 22-line UART stream on the bench board and in the sim (hello + 20 timer-paced ticks + DONE). Instruction-level thumb-oracle cases for the LDAEX family on the M33 remain open. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ behaviorally silicon-anchored 2026-06-11 (instruction oracle open) 38 DBGMCU wired at 0x44024000 (embassy pokes DBGMCU_CR during init) foreign-fw twin IDCODE 0x10016484 from the 2026-06-10 capture; CR is plain R/W storage (cf. #21 F103 finding). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-pinned 2026-06-11 39 FLASH H5 ACR mask 0x133 → 0x13F (LATENCY bits 3:2 were dropped — HAL's latency-5 read-back at 250 MHz failed) foreign-fw twin (Cube) Caught by ST's own HAL example. Bench capture9: ACR write 0x25 reads 0x25, write 0x13F reads 0x13F. My capture6 probes only used latency ≤ 3 — the mask derivation missed the upper bits. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 40 GPDMA per-channel NVIC routing (irq_base, channel n → line 27+n) + window 1KB → 2KB (channel 7's CTR1/CBR1/CSAR/CDAR live above 0x400) + MISR per-channel flag&enable mirror foreign-fw twin (Cube) Caught by ST's HAL example (uses channel 7, IRQ 34; HAL_DMA_IRQHandler gates on MISR which read 0). Bench capture10: ch7 TCIE pends ISPR1 bit 2, transfer byte-exact. Bench capture11: MISR bit 7 = flags&enables live (drops on TCIE clear, returns on re-enable, clears with CFCR). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 41 GPDMA data-handling engine: SDW/DDW width conversion via PAM (zero-pad / sign-extend / left- / right-truncate) + SBX / DBX / DHX exchanges (DmaUnitTransform on DmaRequest) foreign-fw twin (Cube) The seven expected-result vectors from ST's DMA_DataHandling example are encoded as unit tests, and the unmodified example ELF passes all 7 checks on the sim AND on the bench board (LED1 success-blink, error LED dark, observed live over SWD). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-11 (same-ELF twin) 42 GPDMA linked-list mode: node fetch from {CLBAR[31:16], CLLR.LA}, per-U-flag register update (UT1/UT2/UB1/USA/UDA/UT3/UB2/ULL), list-start with CBR1=0, per-block TCF, EN-clear only at list end foreign-fw twin (Cube) Bench capture12 first: a raw 2-block chain over SWD pinned the full lifecycle (both blocks byte-exact, CLLR→0, BNDT=0, CSR=0x301, CCR keeps TCIE with EN cleared). Model fetches nodes as memory→own-register DMA copies. Then ST's DMA_LinkedList example (3-node queue, HAL_DMAEx) reaches its success loop on sim AND board — same ELF, LEDs observed live over SWD. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-12 43 Cortex-M TBB/TBH: branch-table base for Rn=PC is the unaligned in-execution PC (insn+4) — the old Align(PC,4) read tables 2 bytes early at 2-mod-4 addresses foreign-fw twin (Cube) Caught by ST's HAL: HAL_DMA_RegisterCallback's TBB switch dispatched every callback ID into the same slot, so the error callback overwrote the completion callback. Fix per ARMv7-M A7.7.185/186; behavioral anchor = the DMA_LinkedList same-ELF twin. The three ARM Trace Drift Assertions fingerprints legitimately drifted (corrected branch paths) and were re-baselined with all behavioral assertions still green — same precedent as #6. NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ behaviorally silicon-anchored 2026-06-12 44 FDCAN model (H5 fixed message-RAM layout): CCCR INIT/CCE protocol, TEST.LBCK loopback TX→RX FIFO0 through SRAMCAN, IR rc_w1 + TC-gated-by-TXBTIE, TXFQS/RXF0S index machinery, RXF0A ack semantics, CONFIG-block identity (VERR/SIDR) h563-fdcan (tier1 can class) Capture-first (capture13, 2026-06-12): full internal-loopback frame driven over SWD pinned the map (0x84 = XIDAM — the fixed layout, NOT generic Bosch RXF0C), CCE-clears-with-INIT, IR = RF0N|TFE with TC gated by the per-buffer TXBTIE enable, RXF0A advancing F0GI rather than blanking the status. Model rebuilt from the capture (the stranded eb04139f draft had IR at 0x080 colliding RXGFC/TXBC at 0xC0 and generic-Bosch FIFO-config registers that do not exist on H5). Re-proven by h563_class_diff: 22 FDCAN cases, 65/65 total match. tier1 row now 13/13 with the can beachhead class (fixture check_can replays the same sequence). Acceptance filtering, bit timing, FDCAN2 and IT1/ILS routing not modeled (yaml-documented). NUCLEO-H563ZI (bench) ✅ silicon-verified 2026-06-12 45 GDMA peripheral-coupled mode (ESP32-S3): PERI_SEL routing, UART/UHCI0 + SPI2/3 + I2S0/1 coupled byte movement, owner/LEN descriptor writeback (IN unconditional; OUT gated on OUT_AUTO_WRBACK, OUT_CONF0 bit 2), auto-complete fallback for AES/SHA/ADC/RMT/LCD_CAM feat/gdma-peripheral-coupling Same recipe as #4 (JTAG Unity on the bench S3): drive an ESP-IDF DMA driver (uart / spi_master / i2s) on silicon, replay the descriptor sequence in sim, byte-compare buffers + INT_RAW ordering; highest-value probes are the I2S RXEOF_NUM byte semantics and the UART FIFO-idle EOF policy bench ESP32-S3 (proven setup) open

Notes: - Recipes reuse existing machinery only: hw-oracle mmio-diff replays, thumb_oracles, the S3 JTAG Unity loop, F103 capture scripts. No new harnesses required. - Entries #6/#7 are the live example of the rule working — a model fix (#7) tripped a downstream regression snapshot the moment it merged. The original #6 mis-attributed it to a firmware pacing change; clearing the nightly is what surfaced the true cause. - The Trace Drift Assertions gate compares sim-vs-committed-sim fingerprints, so re-baselining after a verified, intentional model fix is correct snapshot maintenance, not a silicon claim — the silicon obligation lives in #7.

docs.title: Silicon-validation roadmap
docs.text: An honest map of how far the simulator-vs-silicon validation goes, and the prioritized path to "you could stake firmware development on sim == silicon." Companion to pending-silicon-verification.md (the per-fix ledger) and ../../hil/README.md (the HIL runner).

docs.title: Where we are
docs.text:
The peripheral-execution oracle (crates/hw-oracle/tests/stm32f1_exec_oracle.rs) runs real ARM code on the full chip bus in sim and on a bench STM32F103 over SWD, then diffs register read-backs. 12 oracles, all _hw/_diff byte-exact, 10 real model bugs fixed across four classes:

Class Examples byte-decomposition (need write_u32) GPIO BSRR atomicity, EXTI SWIER/PR reset / flag values TIM2 UG compare-match flags, RCC_AHBENR=0x14, EXTI SWIER↔PR reserved / width masking AFIO MAPR & EXTICR, CRC_IDR 8-bit on F1/L0 key / write-protection IWDG PR/RLR until KR=0x5555

Plus validations where the model was already right (DMA mem-to-mem, GPIO CRL/CRH, DBGMCU_CR, NVIC ISER/ICER) and the Cortex-M block (NVIC/SCB/DWT) is now wired into the exec bus.

docs.title: Honest gaps (the self-roast)
docs.text:
1. Coverage is a rounding error. ~25 registers on one chip. No %-silicon-validated metric (it would be embarrassingly low).
  2. It's all static. Every oracle is write-register → read-register. We deliberately avoid anything timed — timers counting, UART bit timing, interrupt latency, PLL lock, flash wait states. settle_ticks only checks a DMA end state, never the trajectory.
  3. No interrupt delivery. We validate the NVIC enable registers but never take an interrupt — no exception entry/exit, priority, nesting, or faults.
  4. Halt-mode, not free-running. Run-to-breakpoint over SWD; peripherals can behave differently halted (DBGMCU freeze bits literally change this).
  5. The "silicon-verified" stamp decays. _hw/_diff are #[ignore] and do not run in CI — only the _sim half does, against expectations transcribed from silicon. Nothing catches a new divergence after the bench is unplugged. → HIL-in-CI is the fix (scaffolding landed, inert until a runner is up).
  6. Selection bias. Hand-picked deterministic, safe targets. The 10 bugs are the easy bookkeeping class; zero evidence on clock trees, analog, JIT.
  7. One board, one die. A single (clone-prone) Blue Pill.
  8. One core. STM32F103 only — nothing on Xtensa / RISC-V / nRF / RP2040, all of which have open HW-pending ledger entries.

Verdict: a proven method that cleaned up real bugs — but ~2–3% of the way to trustworthy for F103, ~0% elsewhere.

docs.title: P0 — HIL in CI (stop the decay) — scaffolding done, deploy pending
docs.text: hil/ + core-hil.yml are in place and proven on the bench F103. Deploy a self-hosted runner on the Mac server (hil/README.md), set the board active, enable the schedule: trigger, and make hil a required check only once the runner is reliably online. Every supported chip gets a boards.json entry.

docs.title: P1 — Interrupt delivery oracles — first oracle landed (ledger #23)
docs.text: exti0_interrupt_delivery validates the full path — VTOR relocation, NVIC enable, vectoring, register stacking/unstacking, exception return — byte-exact on the bench F103. The harness gained Thumb::Data, bx/cpsie_i, per-case entry_offset, and opt-in live_peripherals. Next within P1: priority/nesting (two IRQs), fault handlers, SysTick interrupt. The original design notes (now implemented): - CPU built via configure_cortex_m in run_capture (opt-in) so the CPU shares the bus NVIC/VTOR (currently run_capture builds a bare CortexM). - Live-peripheral dispatch (opt-in live_peripherals): after each cpu.step(), call bus.tick_peripherals_fully() and cpu.set_exception_pending(irq) for each returned IRQ — mirroring Machine::step. Default off, so the 12 static oracles are unaffected. - Vector table support: a Thumb::Data(u32) raw-word variant + bx/cpsie_i encoders. Lay out [vector table][isr ending in BX LR][main]; the program sets VTOR via STR to 0xE000ED08 (SCB shares the VTOR Arc) and enables the IRQ via NVIC ISER. The table sits at PROG_BASE_HW (128-byte aligned, VTOR rule); the entry PC is PROG_BASE_HW + table + isr (start of main), so the harness needs a per-case entry offset (sim and HW). main ends at the auto-appended B . terminator (the breakpoint), so the ISR's BX LR returns into main which then settles — no mid-program terminator problem. - First oracle: EXTI0 → ISR sets a RAM marker + clears EXTI_PR; assert the marker (proves the ISR ran) and the unstacked register state. Silicon- verifiable on the connected F103.

docs.title: P2 — Timed oracles — DWT mechanism oracle landed (ledger #24)
docs.text: dwt_cyccnt_advances validates the DWT enable→count→read path with a self-relative boolean (the absolute count diverges — the sim is not cycle-accurate). True cycle-fidelity is a known sim limitation, not closeable by an oracle. DWT is now mapped. Enable DWT_CYCCNT, run a known instruction/delay sequence, compare cycle counts within a tolerance band (sim cycle models aren't cycle-exact). Then timer counts over a fixed delay, and systick reload→COUNTFLAG.

docs.title: P3 — %-silicon-validated metric
docs.text: Extend the register_coverage machinery: per peripheral, registers an oracle asserts on ÷ SVD registers. Commit as a tracked report next to the register-modeling ratchet, so the shallow-coverage critique becomes a number we watch climb.

docs.title: P4 — Free-running (non-halt) validation
docs.text: Generalize the ESP32-S3 JTAG-Unity self-reporting pattern: firmware computes and reports results over UART/RTT while running, no debugger halt. Closes the halt-mode epistemic gap.

docs.title: P5 — Firmware-level differential — v1 landed
docs.text:
firmware-f103-conformance is one bare-metal firmware that drives every peripheral through a realistic sequence and writes an observable-state digest to a fixed RAM block; crates/hw-oracle/tests/f103_conformance.rs runs the same ELF on the full-chip Machine and on silicon (openocd flash + run) and diffs the digests, reporting a conformance % + a per-field gap report, baseline-gated.

First run measured 10/11 (91%) and already paid off — it found gaps the register-poke oracles structurally can't, because real firmware drives registers back-to-back and hits hardware access latencies the sim doesn't model: - CRC reset latency — feeding DR immediately after CR.RESET lost the first word on silicon; a settle() between them fixed it. - TIM2 status-latch latency — reading SR one instruction after EGR.UG read 0 on silicon; the compare-match flags need a cycle to latch. - residual: exti_pr (firmware-context re-pending of EXTI line 0 from the GPIO-driven PA0 — the isolated EXTI oracle confirms the sim value is correct, so this is a hardware artifact, not a modeling gap).

Meta-finding: the sim models no peripheral access/latch latency, so tightly- coded firmware that works in sim can fail on silicon — a modeling-quality dimension invisible to hand-spaced micro-oracles. Next: widen the firmware to SPI/I2C/USART/ADC/RTC/PWR, and grow into the v2 checkpoint register-file diff.

docs.title: P6 — Breadth
docs.text: Other cores (extend the existing Xtensa/RISC-V/nRF oracle banks under HIL), multiple silicon specimens/revs, and fuzzed register sequences to kill the selection bias.
